notes:
võ means martial arts — fighting skills like karate, taekwondo, kung fu, etc.

It’s an uncountable noun in general, but you can say môn võ (a martial art) when talking about a specific style. Common verbs: học võ (learn), tập võ (practice), dạy võ (teach).  

Often appears in võ thuật (martial arts, more formal) or with a specific name like võ Taekwondo, võ cổ truyền (traditional martial arts).

Think of võ as the skill/system, not the match or competition itself.
exampleSentences: Em thích học võ vì nó giúp em khỏe mạnh hơn.
Anh trai anh đã tập võ từ khi còn nhỏ.
Chị muốn đăng ký lớp võ ở trung tâm gần nhà.

translation.en: I study martial arts very diligently.

example.en: I practice martial arts to protect myself.
example.vi: Tôi học võ để bảo vệ bản thân.

definitions.en: A form of combat or self-defense, often involving physical techniques.
definitions.es: Una forma de combate o defensa personal, que a menudo implica técnicas físicas.
definitions.vi: Một hình thức chiến đấu hoặc tự vệ, thường liên quan đến các kỹ thuật thể chất.

translation.en: I practice martial arts to protect myself.

content.sentence: Tôi học ___ để bảo vệ bản thân.

exampleSentencesTranslated: I like learning martial arts because it makes me healthier.
My older brother has practiced martial arts since he was young.
I want to sign up for a martial arts class at the center near my house.
notes_md:
**võ** means *martial arts* — fighting skills like karate, taekwondo, kung fu, etc.

It’s an uncountable noun in general, but you can say **môn võ** (a martial art) when talking about a specific style. Common verbs: **học võ** (learn), **tập võ** (practice), **dạy võ** (teach).  

Often appears in **võ thuật** (martial arts, more formal) or with a specific name like **võ Taekwondo**, **võ cổ truyền** (traditional martial arts).

Think of **võ** as the skill/system, not the match or competition itself.
